the mute insect on the summer pool , That with long cobweb legs , firm as on earth The ostrich skims , flits idly to and fro , Making no dimple on the watery mass ; To the huge grampus , spouting , as he rolls , A cataract , amid the cold clear sky , And furrowing far and wide the northern deep

the breezy void , Dependant on the trembling line he wove , Insidious , and intent on scenes of spoil And death : — So mounts Ambition , and aloft On his proud summit meditates new scenes Of plunder and dominion , till the breeze Of fortune change , that blows to empty air His feeble , frail support , and once again Leaves him a reptile , struggling in the dust

, It was a God That made them : but nor date , nor name Oblivion shows ; cloud only , rolling on , And wrapping darker as it rolls , the works Of man !

The garden-shed , where , at his dinner set , The laboured hind strews here and there a crumb From his brown bread
from his broken tube the smoke ascend On an inverted barrow , as in state He sits , though poor , the monarch of the scene , As pondering deep the garden's future state , His kingdom
with simple skill , With which he hopes to snare the prowling race , The mice , rapacious of his vernal hopes

Comes hopping to his feet : and say , you great , You mighty monarchs of this earthly scene , What nobler views can elevate the heart Of a proud patriot king , than thus to chase The bold rapacious spoilers from the field , And with an eye of merciful regard To look on humble worth , wet from the storm , And chilled by indigence
What nobler views can elevate the heart Of a proud patriot king

Those who can lift their look elate
the voice of charity Pleads like a voice from heaven
unmoved The bloodiest orgies of blood-boltered France
those most gay , and lovely ; and a tear With those most manly
its arm when a false Stuart fled
